contribution.title: Cartan formalism (moving frames, exterior calculus)
contribution.description: Coordinate-free language for differential geometry using differential forms and orthonormal frames.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Modern formulation of general relativity uses Cartan structure equations; useful for tetrad-based numerical relativity.

contribution.title: Einstein-Cartan theory
contribution.description: Extension of general relativity including torsion as well as curvature.
contribution.relevance_to_blackhole_ds: Speculative_extension; useful as a foil for understanding why standard GR uses only curvature.

reasoning_lens.proof_standard: Coordinate-free formulations; expose the geometric content first.
reasoning_lens.favorite_question: What is the differential form that captures this physics?
Can I work in an orthonormal frame to simplify?
reasoning_lens.failure_mode: Cartan's notation is famously dense; can obscure rather than clarify for beginners.

reference.citation: Cartan, E. (1923). Sur les varietes a connexion affine et la theorie de la relativite generalisee. Annales scientifiques de l'E.N.S. 40, 325.

how_to_apply.when_to_consult: Implementing tetrad-based formulations or working with differential forms.
how_to_apply.cautions: Coordinate-free formulations are powerful but pedagogically heavy; document the equivalent coordinate calculation.
